feeds.title: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics
feeds.tldr: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics currently has 4 retained item(s) on the page. Key items include "EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing", "Towards a Cytometry Foundation Model: Interpretable Sample-level Predictive Modelling via Pretrained Transformers", and "TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data".

Towards a Cytometry Foundation Model: Interpretable Sample-level Predictive Modelling via Pretrained Transformers
TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data

feeds.title: Cell
feeds.tldr: Cell currently has 1 retained item(s) on the page. Key items include "Senescence in cancer: Hallmarks, paradoxes, and therapeutic promise".
highlights: Senescence in cancer: Hallmarks, paradoxes, and therapeutic promise

items.title: Senescence in cancer: Hallmarks, paradoxes, and therapeutic promise
items.tldr: Cellular senescence, defined by six major hallmarks, is a program that halts cell division while rewiring chromatin, metabolism, microenvironment sensing, and immune interactions to either suppress or promote cancer and is an exciting frontier for precision t…

feeds.title: Nature Communications
feeds.tldr: This digest presents ten recent papers from Nature Communications covering diverse fields such as biomedical informatics, developmental biology, climate science, and energy technology. Key contributions include a framework for secure electronic health record sharing, insights into lung progenitor dynamics, and innovations in hybrid energy systems. Additional studies address permafrost thaw variability, solid-state battery manufacturing, primordial protein design, plasma probing techniques, AI in agriculture, cancer replication mechanisms, and HIV prevention outcomes.
highlights: Cross-institutional EHR harmonization without data sharing
SOX9+ lung progenitors control size and cell fate
PV-TE hybrid systems achieve over 50% efficiency

items.title: Representation learning to advance multi-institutional studies with electronic health record data from US and France
items.tldr: A framework integrates medical knowledge, large language models, and graph learning to harmonize electronic health records across hospitals, enabling cross-institutional analysis without sharing patient-level data.

items.title: Hippo signaling differentially regulates distal progenitor subpopulations and their transitional states to construct the mammalian lungs
items.tldr: Distal SOX9+ lung progenitors control lung size through bud bifurcation and undergo transitional states to generate conducting airway and alveolar cell types.

items.title: Permafrost landsystems define regional variability in climate change effects on northern environments
items.tldr: Permafrost landsystems, shaped by landform-ecosystem co-development and climatic legacies, predict regional thaw consequences across ecoclimate gradients.

items.title: Mechanofusion-derived cathode composite microstructures with scalable mixed conducting matrix coatings for solid state batteries
items.tldr: Solvent-free mechanofusion applies mixed conducting matrix coatings to cathode particles, with quality linked to mixing stresses for scalable solid-state battery manufacturing.

items.title: A highly limited amino acid library from asteroid Bennu yields wide-ranging protein folds
items.tldr: AI-based protein design shows that small, primordial amino acid libraries from asteroid data can form a broad range of protein folds.

items.title: Probing ultrafast heating and ionization dynamics in solid density plasmas with time-resolved resonant X-ray absorption and emission
items.tldr: Resonant x-ray emission and absorption imaging from an XFEL probe achieve sub-picosecond resolution of heating and ionization in solid-density plasmas.

items.title: Solution-processed photovoltaic and thermoelectric hybrid systems with efficiency exceeding 50%
items.tldr: Optimized solution-processed photovoltaic-thermoelectric hybrid systems demonstrate a maximum efficiency of 50.28%.

items.title: A conversational multi-agent AI system for automated plant phenotyping
items.tldr: A conversational AI system integrates large language models with phenotyping tools to automate crop analysis and enhance accessibility.

items.title: Distinct mechanisms of replication stress induced by oncogenic RAS and cyclin E1 converge on R-loop-dependent fork reversal
items.tldr: Oncogenic RAS induces replication fork stalling via ROS-driven replisome alterations, leading to R-loop-dependent fork reversal.

items.title: Influence of the broadly neutralizing antibody VRC01 on HIV breakthrough virus populations in antibody-mediated prevention trials
items.tldr: Deep sequencing of AMP trial participants reveals high multilineage HIV infections and coinfection, with VRC01 not affecting the transmission bottleneck.

feeds.title: Nature Computational Science
feeds.tldr: This issue of Nature Computational Science includes two studies. One introduces HorusEye, a self-supervised foundation model that learns noise from X-ray scans for robust tomography restoration across diverse settings without clean training data. The other presents a benchmark for spatial alignment in transcriptomics, finding that the best method is dataset dependent and providing guidance for future development.
highlights: HorusEye enables universal tomography restoration via self-supervised noise learning without clean data.
Spatial transcriptomics alignment performance varies by dataset, per benchmark analysis.
Benchmarking offers actionable insights for method development in computational biology.

items.title: Universal restoration of medical images
items.tldr: A self-supervised foundation model named HorusEye learns realistic noise directly from X-ray scans to enable robust tomography restoration across diverse modalities, scanners, and tasks without requiring clean training data.

items.title: Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data
items.tldr: A benchmark study evaluates spatial alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics and shows that the best approach is dataset dependent, offering actionable guidance for future method development.

feeds.title: Nature Health
feeds.tldr: This digest includes a Nature Health paper discussing GLP-1 receptor antagonists as potential longevity therapeutics. The paper classifies them as transitional but calls for more rigorous testing and evidence to assess their direct and indirect effects.
highlights: GLP-1 receptor antagonists in longevity research
Need for more evidence on effects

items.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists should be rigorously tested as longevity therapeutics
items.tldr: GLP-1 receptor antagonists are classified as transitional longevity therapeutics, but more evidence is needed to assess their direct and indirect effects.

feeds.title: Nature Medicine
feeds.tldr: This Nature Medicine digest includes three papers. One reports the detection of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo with co-circulation of multiple clades. Another examines exposome factors across 34 countries, linking social exposures to faster functional brain aging and physical exposures to faster structural brain aging. The third outlines target product profiles for therapies to delay or prevent symptomatic Alzheimer's disease.
highlights: Monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 found in Congo with co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb.
Global exposome study: social exposures accelerate functional brain aging, physical exposures accelerate structural brain aging.
Target product profiles proposed to guide development of Alzheimer's disease prevention therapies.

items.title: Evidence of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clade Ia, Ib and clade IIb
items.tldr: Whole-genome phylogenetic analysis identified a case of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of three clade lineages, emphasizing the need for improved surveillance due to potential recombination risks.

items.title: The exposome of brain aging across 34 countries
items.tldr: Exposome analyses across 34 countries showed that social exposures are associated with faster functional brain aging, while physical exposures are associated with faster structural brain aging.

items.title: Target product profiles for treatments to delay or prevent symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease
items.tldr: Authors outline target product profiles for therapies designed to delay or prevent the onset of clinical symptoms of Alzheimer's disease, aiming to accelerate therapeutic development and provide clear benchmarks.

feeds.title: Nature Microbiology
feeds.tldr: This digest covers three studies: a metagenomic analysis revealing extensive hidden prokaryotic diversity in unbinned contigs, the discovery of an endonuclease family that blocks nanotube-mediated plasmid transfer in bacteria, and findings that cholesterol overload in tuberculosis granulomas disrupts macrophage-CD4+ T cell interactions, impairing infection control.
highlights: Analysis of 92,187 metagenomes predicts ~705,000 bacterial and ~27,000 archaeal species-level clades, mostly from unbinned contigs, with soils and aquatic environments as diversity hotspots.
YokF endonuclease in Bacillus subtilis acts as a donor-controlled barrier to plasmid transfer via nanotubes, with homologues widespread in Gram-positive bacteria.
Spatial transcriptomics shows infection-driven cholesterol accumulation in TB granulomas inhibits macrophage-CD4+ T cell interactions, compromising anti-tuberculosis immunity.

items.title: Unbinned contigs expand known diversity in the global microbiome
items.tldr: A comprehensive cross-habitat metagenome dataset indicates that most discoverable bacterial and archaeal diversity is hidden among unbinned contigs, predicting hundreds of thousands of new species-level clades and identifying soils and aquatic environments as hotspots, with clade size distributions following power laws.

items.title: A family of endonucleases blocks nanotube-mediated plasmid exchange
items.tldr: The YokF endonuclease provides a donor-controlled mechanism to limit plasmid transfer to recipient bacteria via nanotubes in Bacillus subtilis, with functional homologues identified across Gram-positive bacterial species.

items.title: Lipid accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas inhibits macrophage–CD4 + T cell interactions and infection control
items.tldr: Spatial transcriptomics and immunofluorescence microscopy of human and mouse samples reveal that infection-driven cholesterol overload in tuberculosis granulomas impairs macrophage-CD4+ T cell interactions and anti-tuberculosis immunity.
